title: Input parameter nodes for user-editable DSL values
description: Replace the float_math(operation=ADD, a=X, b=0.0) hack for user-editable values with proper input parameter nodes. These are the values the UI will expose as sliders, checkboxes, and spinners when the user drills into a DSL subtree. New nodes: input_float(name, default, min, max), input_int(name, default, min, max), input_boolean(name, default). float_curve already serves this role for curve parameters. All share a common 'user-editable input' semantic. The node graph runtime should be able to enumerate all input parameter nodes in a graph to build a UI parameter panel automatically.
definition-of-done: 1. input_float, input_int, input_boolean nodes exist with name, default, min, max (where applicable) inputs. 2. Each outputs a single value on its 'result' port. 3. NodeGraphRuntime can enumerate all input parameter nodes in a parsed graph (names, types, defaults, ranges). 4. float_curve is documented as part of the same input-parameter family. 5. tool_quilt.dsl compiles with the new input nodes replacing the float_math hacks. 6. All existing mesh tests pass.
testing-plan: 1. Write a unit test that parses a DSL with input_float, input_int, input_boolean nodes and verifies the runtime can enumerate them with correct metadata. 2. Verify tool_quilt.dsl still validates and executes after replacing float_math hacks with input nodes. 3. Verify that overriding an input value at runtime (e.g., changing subdivisions) produces the expected mesh difference.
todos: Create InputIntNode (or verify existing one has name/default/min/max)
Add enumeration API to NodeGraphRuntime for discovering input parameter nodes
Update tool_quilt.dsl to use input nodes instead of float_math hacks
Verify float_curve fits the same pattern and document it
unknowns: Whether min/max should be enforced at evaluation time or just metadata for UI
Whether input nodes need a 'group' or 'category' field for UI organization
